message:
fix(chatgpt,bd-srzwm): refuse permissive modes + symlinks on AES key file

Pre-fix: load_encryption_key called fs::read() on the chatgpt key
file path with NO check on permissions or symlink status. A
32-byte AES-256 key file with mode 0644 (world-readable) or 0664
(group-readable) was loaded silently. SSH refuses to use a private
key with mode > 0600 for exactly this reason — the key decrypts the
operator's full ChatGPT conversation history (potentially years of
personal/professional content); on a shared dev box where another
user can read ~/.config/cass/, the key leaks.

Symlinks at the key file path were also accepted; a symlink to
/dev/null or /etc/passwd would silently load the wrong bytes as a
"key" — bad UX (decrypt fails downstream with a confusing error)
AND a TOCTOU surface (the symlink target can change between stat
and read).

Post-fix: new helper chatgpt_key_file_mode_is_safe(path) is called
before fs::read in the file-path loop. It:
  - calls fs::symlink_metadata (does NOT follow symlinks)
  - returns false (with tracing::warn) if the path is a symlink
  - returns false (with tracing::warn) if mode & 0o077 != 0
    (i.e., any group/other read/write/exec bit is set)
  - emits a chmod hint pointing at the offending path
The Windows arm is a no-op (mode bits don't carry the same meaning;
ChatGPT desktop encryption is macOS-only anyway).

Two regression tests:
- chatgpt_key_file_mode_is_safe_refuses_permissive_modes — pins
  0600/0400 accept, 0644/0660 refuse.
- chatgpt_key_file_mode_is_safe_refuses_symlinks — pins symlink
  refusal even when the target is a regular 0600 file.

Validation: rch test pass via cass path-dep build, exit=0.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-srzwm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
